paragraphs.text: party of men engaged in craw-fishing
relate to the San Diego Union a strange tale of earthquakes, winds, storms and meteorological disturbances which occurred there about the first of July.
, the only little harbor possessed by
, was completely filled up with sand during these disturbances. Speaking of
is the most westerly of the group known as the
, and is about 150 miles distant from
. Twenty-five years ago it was covered with grass, which afforded pasture for thousands of sheep, and its owners lived there in a fine residence.

"A strange transformation has been going on since then. It is now a windy, barren, sandy waste, where but a few hundred sheep, without a shepherd, eke out a miserable existence. It is but eight miles long and four wide, but wherever one digs can be found human bones and skulls. In the distant past it was either densely populated, or a burial ground, or a great battlefield.
